CheckTitle: AI agent must be scoped to a single data domain
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Checks that each AI agent is authorized to access only a single data domain (e.g. only S3, or only RDS, or only DynamoDB). Agents that span multiple data domains accumulate a wider blast radius and complicate data-lineage governance, GDPR data-subject access requests, and cross-domain policy enforcement.
Risk: An agent with permissions across multiple data domains can be weaponized to exfiltrate data from several systems simultaneously. Cross-domain access also breaks data-classification boundaries — an agent with access to both a public S3 bucket and an encrypted RDS instance can act as a bridge to move data between classification levels.

Code.Other:
1. Review the agent manifest `data_domains_accessed` field to identify all domains in use.
2. For each domain beyond the first, assess whether the agent genuinely needs that access for its declared purpose.
3. Split the agent into domain-scoped agents (one per domain) if multiple data domains are legitimately required.
4. Update IAM policies to restrict each agent role to resources within its single data domain.
5. Add resource-based policies (S3 bucket policies, RDS resource policies) to deny cross-agent access.
6. Update the agent manifest to reflect the new single-domain scope.

Recommendation.Text: Design AI agents around a single data domain. Where an orchestrator needs to coordinate across domains, use separate sub-agents with narrow data-domain permissions and have the orchestrator aggregate results. Apply resource-based policies (S3 bucket policies, KMS key policies) to enforce domain boundaries in addition to IAM policies.

Notes: ASPM-020 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
